summary_one_line: Input must encode a CLUT grid dimension <= 1 so CubeSize takes the dim<=1 error-return true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A parsed ICC profile whose CLUT element supplies a per-axis grid-point count clutPoints[b-1] of 0 or 1, so dim<=1 holds inside CubeSize. The winning seeds carry alternate profile-class/colorspace tags (e.g. 'lams'/'link', '8CLR', 'MCH8Lab') and a flipped flag byte 01 at offset 0x2d that route to an MPE CLUT stage with a degenerate dimension, whereas a normal 'mntr'/'RGB ' display profile fills full-size dimensions.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving vpc seeds reach the blocker true side: at line 470 the per-role overlay shows the call-chain branch with W counts T=7 F=18 (true side hit), and the caller's n==0 path (lines 577-580) is W-only, meaning CubeSize returned 0 because a dimension was <=1. The vpc seeds differ at the colorspace/tag offsets (0x0c-0x16) and at 0x2d=01, structuring a profile that allocates a CLUT with a unit/zero axis.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser value_profile seeds never take the true side: at line 470 L counts are T=0 F=120, i.e. the dim<=1 test is reached 120 times but always falls through with dim>1. The loser seeds hold the standard 'lcms'/'mntr'/'RGB ' display-profile tags and 00 at 0x2d, producing full-size CLUT dimensions so dim never drops to <=1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers in the pair share value_profile's distance-bucket comparison feedback; the only delta is value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace stage. The degenerate dimension byte is a discrete small comparand reached only after the colorspace/tag bytes pass earlier equality CMPs; I2SRandReplace splices logged comparand values into the exact input-byte positions, letting vpc set the tag/dimension bytes that drive dim<=1, which value_profile without I2S substitution cannot synthesize.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the dim<=1 error-return whose globally-blocked side is the true branch.
value_profile_cmplog wins (8/10 resolved) over value_profile (0/10) and the pair delta is I2S.
The winner takes the true side of the blocker while the loser never does (L T=0 F=120 at line 470).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 470  T=7 F=18  T=0 F=120  if (dim <= 1) return 0;  // Error

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-only caller path is the n==0 return, evidence that CubeSize returned 0 via a degenerate dimension.
Winner and loser seeds differ at the colorspace/tag bytes that select the CLUT-bearing profile structure.
Winner seeds set flag byte 01 at offset 0x2d where losers hold 00.
Loser value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; only the winner vpc has the I2S substitution stage.
value_profile uses CMP_MAP gradient feedback but has no I2SRandReplace, isolating I2S as the pair delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2197 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed f8ca52c60487cd68` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 13 of the 14-level ancestor chain, the floor signal for an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the dim<=1 axis is gated only behind a CMP_MAP-reachable gradient (no discrete tag/dimension comparand to substitute) and value_profile resolves it as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing the I2S substitution is not what supplies the degenerate dimension.

weakest_evidence_point: The link from the differing tag/0x2d bytes to a specific clutPoints axis value of 0 or 1 is inferred from source structure and per-role counts, not directly confirmed by a parsed dimension value.
